lit.usageNote: In Black American English, often means excited, excellent, or intoxicated.
lit.culturalNote: Widely used in North American youth and Black vernacular.

fam.usageNote: Short for family; used to address close friends or crew.
fam.culturalNote: Common in Black English and hip-hop culture.

rizz.usageNote: Charisma, charm, or ability to attract others. Short for 'charisma.'
rizz.culturalNote: Exploded on TikTok; often 'rizz up' or 'pull rizz.'

sigma.usageNote: A lone-wolf type who succeeds without seeking approval; from 'sigma male' meme.
sigma.culturalNote: Part of alpha/beta/sigma internet masculinity slang.

gyatt.usageNote: Exclamation of surprise or approval; often used reactively.
gyatt.culturalNote: Spread via streaming and Gen Alpha platforms.

skibidi.usageNote: Nonsense word from viral Skibidi Toilet series; used for humor or randomness.
skibidi.culturalNote: Gen Alpha internet culture, YouTube Shorts.

fanum tax.usageNote: When someone takes a bite of your food; from streamer Fanum.

slay.usageNote: To do something exceptionally well; to look amazing.
slay.culturalNote: From ballroom and Black queer culture; mainstream Gen Z.

bussin.usageNote: Really good, especially food. 'This is bussin.'
bussin.culturalNote: Black American English, spread to Gen Z broadly.

no cap.usageNote: No lie; for real. 'No cap, that was fire.'
no cap.example: No cap, that was the best game ever.
no cap.etymologyJourney: From cap meaning "lie" (early 1900s); "no cap" = no lie, for real, in 2010s Black American English and then mainstream.

cap.usageNote: A lie or something false. 'That's cap.'
cap.culturalNote: Black American English; opposite of 'no cap.'

bet.usageNote: Okay, agreed, or 'I'm in.' Used as confirmation.
bet.culturalNote: Common in North American youth and Black English.

drip.culturalNote: Hip-hop and Black English; 'drip' = what you're wearing.

vibe.usageNote: The mood or feeling of a situation; to relax or get along. 'Good vibes.'
vibe.culturalNote: Widespread; roots in Black and hippie usage.

stan.usageNote: To be an obsessive fan (noun or verb). From Eminem's 'Stan.'
stan.culturalNote: Internet fandom; used across Gen Z and Millennial.

ghost.usageNote: To disappear from someone's life or ignore messages (ghosting).
ghost.culturalNote: Dating and social media; Millennial/Gen Z.

tea.usageNote: Gossip or juicy information. 'Spill the tea.'
tea.culturalNote: From Black queer and drag culture.

woke.usageNote: Aware of social injustice; sometimes used ironically.
woke.culturalNote: Black American English; broad political and ironic use.

period.usageNote: Emphasis: 'And that's that, period.' End of discussion.
period.culturalNote: Black English; used for finality or agreement.

fire.usageNote: Really good, hot, or excellent. 'That track is fire.'
fire.culturalNote: Hip-hop and Black English; mainstream Gen Z.

lowkey.usageNote: Kind of, a little bit; or kept quiet. 'Lowkey obsessed.'

highkey.usageNote: Obviously, very much; not hiding it. 'Highkey need this.'

yeet.usageNote: To throw something with force; or an exclamation of excitement.
yeet.culturalNote: Viral Vine/origin meme; Gen Z staple.

simp.usageNote: Someone who goes overboard to please another person (often romantically).

sus.usageNote: Suspicious; from Among Us. 'That's kinda sus.'

based.usageNote: Authentic, not caring what others think; or agreeing strongly.
based.culturalNote: From Lil B; internet and Gen Z.

mid.usageNote: Mediocre, average, not great. 'That was mid.'
mid.culturalNote: Black English and gaming; Gen Z.

slaps.usageNote: Really good, especially music. 'This song slaps.'
slaps.culturalNote: Widespread Gen Z; music and food.

go off.usageNote: To do something really well or to rant. 'She went off.'

deadass.culturalNote: Black American English; New York influence.

bestie.usageNote: Best friend; used affectionately or ironically.
bestie.culturalNote: Social media and Gen Z friendship language.

salty.usageNote: Upset, bitter, or annoyed. 'Don't be salty.'
salty.culturalNote: Gaming and general slang; Millennial/Gen Z.

squad.culturalNote: Black English and social media; Gen Z/Millennial.

flex.usageNote: To show off money, status, or something. 'Flex on them.'

adulting.usageNote: Doing grown-up tasks (bills, chores). 'I'm adulting today.'

fomo.usageNote: Fear of missing out. Anxiety about not being where the action is.

yolo.usageNote: You only live once. Used to justify a risky or fun choice.

hangry.usageNote: Angry because you're hungry. Hungry + angry.

ship.usageNote: To support a romantic pairing (relationship). 'I ship them.'

basic.usageNote: Mainstream, unoriginal, or predictable. 'Basic white girl.'

cancel.usageNote: To reject or boycott someone (cancel culture).

whatever.usageNote: Dismissive; I don't care. Often drawn out: 'Whatev-er.'

chill.usageNote: Relax; or cool, laid-back. 'Let's chill.' 'He's chill.'
chill.culturalNote: Gen X and hip-hop; still used by Millennials and Gen Z.

buzzkill.usageNote: Someone or something that ruins the mood or fun.
buzzkill.culturalNote: Gen X and Boomer; party and drug culture origins.

poser.usageNote: Someone who pretends to be something they're not; fake fan.
poser.culturalNote: Punk and alternative Gen X culture.

sellout.usageNote: Someone who abandons principles for money or mainstream success.
sellout.culturalNote: Punk and indie Gen X ethos.

meh.culturalNote: Simpsons origin; Gen X and internet.

cool.usageNote: Good, approved, fashionable. The all-purpose positive.
cool.culturalNote: Jazz and Beat era; Boomer and lasting.

dig.usageNote: To like or understand. 'I dig it.'

groovy.usageNote: Cool, great, in the groove. 'That's groovy.'

far out.usageNote: Amazing, mind-blowing, or weird in a good way.

hip.usageNote: In the know, fashionable. 'Hip to the scene.'
hip.culturalNote: Jazz and Beat; Boomer era; root of 'hippie.'

ohio.usageNote: Meme term for something weird, surreal, or off. 'That's so Ohio.'
ohio.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha; Ohio as absurdist punchline.

grimace shake.usageNote: Viral McDonald's purple shake; meme about it being cursed or weird.
grimace shake.culturalNote: 2023 TikTok trend; Gen Alpha viral marketing meme.

main character.usageNote: You or someone as the star of their own story. 'Main character energy.'
main character.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha self-identity; main character moment.

npc.usageNote: Non-player character; someone basic, following a script, or not unique.
npc.culturalNote: Gaming term; Gen Alpha uses for people who lack personality.

touch grass.usageNote: Go outside; get a life. Used when someone is too online.
touch grass.culturalNote: Internet insult; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

banger.usageNote: A really good song or track. 'This is a banger.'
banger.culturalNote: Music slang; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

ratio.usageNote: When replies outnumber likes (you got ratioed = L). Or to ratio someone.
ratio.culturalNote: Twitter/X and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

cringe.usageNote: Embarrassing, awkward, or hard to watch. 'That's cringe.'
cringe.culturalNote: Internet and streaming; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

vibe check.usageNote: Checking if someone or something has good or bad vibes.
vibe check.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha; passing or failing the vibe check.

pog.usageNote: Awesome, exciting. From Twitch emote; reaction to something cool.
pog.culturalNote: Streaming and gaming; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

poggers.usageNote: Same as pog; very cool or hype. 'That's poggers.'

copium.usageNote: False hope or cope; denial. 'That's copium.'
copium.culturalNote: Meme from Twitch; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

no shot.usageNote: No way; zero chance. 'There's no shot that happens.'

ate.usageNote: Killed it; did amazingly. 'She ate.' 'She ate that up.'
ate.culturalNote: Black English and ballroom; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

left no crumbs.usageNote: Did something perfectly; left nothing for anyone else. 'She left no crumbs.'
left no crumbs.culturalNote: Black English and drag; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

it's giving.usageNote: It has the vibe of; it reminds me of. 'It's giving main character.'
it's giving.culturalNote: Black queer and ballroom; TikTok and Gen Alpha.

ick.usageNote: A turn-off; something that kills attraction. 'The ick.'
ick.culturalNote: Dating and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

delulu.usageNote: Delusional; used playfully. 'Delulu is the solulu.'
delulu.culturalNote: K-pop and internet fandom; Gen Alpha.

skill issue.usageNote: Your fault for being bad; git gud. 'That's a skill issue.'
skill issue.culturalNote: Gaming and Twitch; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

mald.usageNote: To be mad; malding = getting upset. From 'mad' + 'bald.'

seethe.usageNote: To be angry and bitter. 'Stay mad, seethe.'
seethe.culturalNote: Internet and 4chan; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

situationship.usageNote: A relationship that's not defined; more than friends, not official.
situationship.culturalNote: Dating and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

ick energy.usageNote: Vibes that give someone the ick; off-putting.

she's a 10 but.usageNote: Meme format: someone is perfect but has one flaw. 'She's a 10 but she's Ohio.'
she's a 10 but.culturalNote: TikTok trend; Gen Alpha rating format.

hot girl walk.usageNote: A walk for yourself; self-care, no male gaze. From Megan Thee Stallion.
hot girl walk.culturalNote: TikTok wellness trend; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

girl dinner.usageNote: A random snack plate instead of a real meal. 'This is my girl dinner.'
girl dinner.culturalNote: TikTok trend; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

girl math.usageNote: Joke logic for spending: 'If I use a gift card it's free.'
girl math.culturalNote: TikTok trend; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

boy math.usageNote: Joke logic for men's bad takes or behavior. Counter to girl math.
boy math.culturalNote: TikTok trend; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

gatekeep.usageNote: To keep something from others; 'don't gatekeep' = share it.
gatekeep.culturalNote: Fandom and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

gaslight.usageNote: To manipulate someone into doubting reality. 'Stop gaslighting me.'
gaslight.culturalNote: Psychology term; widespread Gen Alpha and Gen Z use.

girlboss.usageNote: Female entrepreneur; often used ironically after the term was criticized.
girlboss.culturalNote: Millennial origin; Gen Alpha uses ironically.

soft launch.usageNote: Hinting at a relationship or news without saying it outright.
soft launch.culturalNote: Social media and dating; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

hard launch.usageNote: Officially announcing a relationship or news to everyone.
hard launch.culturalNote: Social media; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

beige flag.usageNote: A weird but not deal-breaking trait. Between green and red flag.

green flag.usageNote: A good sign in a person or relationship.
green flag.culturalNote: Dating and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

red flag.usageNote: A warning sign; something bad about a person or situation.
red flag.culturalNote: Dating and therapy speak; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

pick me.usageNote: Someone who tries too hard to be chosen; often used for women who put others down.
pick me.culturalNote: TikTok and social media; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

unalive.usageNote: To die or kill; used to avoid content filters on platforms.
unalive.culturalNote: YouTube and TikTok moderation; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

seggs.usageNote: Sex; used to avoid filters or say it in a joking way.
seggs.culturalNote: Platform moderation; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

le dollar bean.usageNote: Lesbian; used to avoid filters or as a joke.
le dollar bean.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha; filter avoidance.

corn.usageNote: Porn; used to avoid content filters.

lettuce.usageNote: Let us; 'lettuce go' = let us go. Playful spelling.
lettuce.culturalNote: Gen Alpha and kids; internet spelling memes.

nip nops.usageNote: Nipples; used to avoid filters or say it in a silly way.

grape.usageNote: Rape; used to avoid filters when discussing the topic seriously or in memes.
grape.culturalNote: Platform moderation; heavy topic, use with care.

rizzler.usageNote: Someone with a lot of rizz; charismatic. 'He's a rizzler.'

rizz lord.usageNote: Someone with maximum rizz; the best at charm.
rizz lord.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha; rizz variation.

breadcrumb.usageNote: A small hint or clue someone leaves; breadcrumbing = stringing along.
breadcrumb.culturalNote: Dating and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

breadcrumbing.usageNote: Leading someone on with small attention; not fully committing.
breadcrumbing.culturalNote: Dating slang; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

alpha male.usageNote: Dominant, confident man; from alpha/beta/sigma meme ecosystem.
alpha male.culturalNote: Internet masculinity; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

beta.usageNote: Submissive or uncool; opposite of alpha in the alpha/beta/sigma meme.

main character energy.usageNote: Vibes of being the star of your own story; confident, memorable.
main character energy.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha; main character variation.

grass toucher.usageNote: Someone who touches grass; has a life outside the internet. Ironic praise.
grass toucher.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

L.usageNote: Loss; you took an L. Something bad happened.
L.culturalNote: Gaming and sports; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

W.usageNote: Win; you got a W. Something good happened.
W.culturalNote: Gaming and sports; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

ratioed.usageNote: Got more replies than likes; your take failed. 'You got ratioed.'

L take.usageNote: A bad opinion. 'That's an L take.'
L take.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

W take.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

big W.usageNote: A major win. 'That's a big W.'

big L.usageNote: A major loss or fail. 'Took a big L.'

periodt.usageNote: Emphasis; that's final. 'She's the best, periodt.'
periodt.culturalNote: Black English; Gen Alpha and Gen Z; spelling of period.

slay queen.usageNote: Praise for someone killing it. 'Slay queen!'
slay queen.culturalNote: Ballroom and Black queer; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

no printer.usageNote: No cap; no lie. 'That's facts, no printer.' (No printer = no receipt = no proof needed.)
no printer.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Alpha; variation of no cap.

print.culturalNote: TikTok; Gen Alpha; from no printer meme.

slaps different.usageNote: Hits differently; hits hard. 'This song slaps different.'
slaps different.culturalNote: Music and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

shooketh.usageNote: Very shocked. Jokingly formal version of shook.
shooketh.culturalNote: Internet and Gen Alpha; Shakespeare meme.

sigma grindset.usageNote: Mindset of a sigma; lone wolf, grind, no approval needed.
sigma grindset.culturalNote: Meme from sigma male; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

delulu is the solulu.usageNote: Being delusional is the solution; playful embrace of denial.

skill diff.usageNote: Short for skill difference; you're just worse. 'Skill diff.'
skill diff.culturalNote: Gaming; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

seething.usageNote: Being visibly angry and bitter. 'They're seething.'
seething.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

hot girl summer.usageNote: Summer of confidence, fun, and self-care. From Megan Thee Stallion.
hot girl summer.culturalNote: 2019 trend; Gen Alpha and Gen Z still use it.

pov.usageNote: Point of view; used to label TikTok skits. 'POV: you just got the ick.'
pov.culturalNote: TikTok format; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

ngl.usageNote: Not gonna lie. 'Ngl that was fire.'
ngl.culturalNote: Text and speech; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

tbh.usageNote: To be honest. 'Tbh I don't care.'
tbh.culturalNote: Text and speech; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

imo.culturalNote: Text and internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

irl.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

rizz god.usageNote: Someone with ultimate rizz; maximum charm.

main character moment.usageNote: A moment when you feel like the star of your own movie.

npc energy.usageNote: Vibes of someone with no personality; basic, scripted.

ohio moment.usageNote: A weird or surreal moment. 'That was such an Ohio moment.'

L + ratio.usageNote: You lost and got ratioed; double L. 'L + ratio + no one asked.'
L + ratio.culturalNote: Twitter and TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

no one asked.usageNote: Dismissive; nobody asked for your opinion.
no one asked.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

she ate that.usageNote: She killed it; did amazing. 'She ate that performance.'
she ate that.culturalNote: Ballroom and Black English; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

zero crumbs.usageNote: Left nothing; did it all. Same as left no crumbs.

cringe fest.usageNote: Something very cringe; full of awkward moments.

vibe killer.usageNote: Someone or something that ruins the vibe.
vibe killer.culturalNote: TikTok; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

pog champ.usageNote: Twitch emote for hype; something pog. 'That's pog champ.'

W energy.usageNote: Winning energy; good vibes. 'She has W energy.'

L energy.usageNote: Losing energy; bad vibes. 'That's L energy.'

slay all day.usageNote: Praise; you're killing it constantly. 'Slay all day.'

that's so slay.usageNote: That's amazing; that slays. 'That's so slay.'

ick list.usageNote: List of things that give you the ick in dating.

it's giving main character.usageNote: It has main character energy. 'It's giving main character.'
it's giving main character.culturalNote: TikTok; Gen Alpha; it's giving + main character.

grimace shake challenge.usageNote: Viral trend of filming yourself after drinking the Grimace shake.

touch grass challenge.usageNote: Ironic challenge to go outside; or actually going outside to touch grass.
touch grass challenge.culturalNote: Internet meme; Gen Alpha and Gen Z.

clout.usageNote: Fame, attention, or influence (especially on social media). 'Doing it for clout.'
clout.culturalNote: Hip-hop and social media; Gen Z.

hater.usageNote: Someone who criticizes or envies you. 'Haters gonna hate.'

bougie.usageNote: Bourgeois; fancy, high-class, or extra. 'That's so bougie.'
bougie.culturalNote: From bourgeois; Black English and Gen Z.

cheugy.usageNote: Outdated, uncool, or trying too hard. 'That's cheugy.'
cheugy.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Z; describes Millennial cringe.

fit check.usageNote: Showing off your outfit; fit pic. 'Fit check.'
fit check.culturalNote: Social media and fashion; Gen Z.

oomf.usageNote: One of my followers/friends; someone I know online. 'Oomf posted this.'
oomf.culturalNote: Twitter and social media; Gen Z.

oomfie.usageNote: Cute version of oomf; one of my followers/friends.

moots.usageNote: Mutuals; people who follow each other. 'We're moots.'
moots.culturalNote: Twitter and social media; Gen Z.

mutuals.usageNote: People you follow who follow you back; mutual followers.

slide.usageNote: To DM someone; slide into DMs. 'I'm gonna slide.'
slide.culturalNote: Dating and social media; Gen Z.

left on read.usageNote: When someone reads your message but doesn't reply. 'I got left on read.'

double text.usageNote: To text again before they reply; seen as desperate. 'I don't double text.'

ghosted.usageNote: When someone cuts off contact with no explanation. 'He ghosted me.'

ghosting.usageNote: Ignoring someone completely; disappearing on them.

vibing.usageNote: Relaxing, going with the flow, or enjoying. 'Just vibing.'

extra.usageNote: Over the top; too much. 'She's so extra.'
extra.culturalNote: Black English and mainstream; Gen Z.

pressed.usageNote: Upset, stressed, or bothered. 'Why are you so pressed?'

triggered.usageNote: Upset or offended; often used dismissively. 'You're triggered.'
triggered.culturalNote: Internet and meme culture; Gen Z.

messy.culturalNote: Reality TV and social media; Gen Z.

receipt.usageNote: Proof; evidence of what someone said or did. 'Show me the receipt.'
receipt.culturalNote: Black English and drag; Gen Z.

receipts.usageNote: Proof or evidence; screenshots, receipts. 'Where are the receipts?'
receipts.culturalNote: Drag and social media; Gen Z.

spill.usageNote: To share tea or gossip. 'Spill!' 'Spill the tea.'
spill.culturalNote: Black queer and drag; Gen Z.

doxxing.usageNote: Publishing someone's private info online to harass them.
doxxing.culturalNote: Internet culture; Gen Z and Millennial.

doxxed.usageNote: Had your private info exposed online. 'I got doxxed.'

subtweet.usageNote: A tweet about someone without naming them. 'That's a subtweet.'

subtweeting.usageNote: Posting about someone indirectly. 'Stop subtweeting me.'

main.usageNote: Your main account or main person. 'That's my main.'
main.culturalNote: Social media and fandom; Gen Z.

link.usageNote: A romantic or sexual connection; someone you're talking to. 'Who's your link?'
link.culturalNote: Dating and Black English; Gen Z.

links.usageNote: Romantic or sexual connections; options. 'She has links.'

bop.usageNote: A catchy, good song. 'This is a bop.'

curve.usageNote: To reject someone; turn them down. 'She curved him.'
curve.culturalNote: Black English and dating; Gen Z.

curving.usageNote: Rejecting someone or giving them the cold shoulder.

zesty.usageNote: Flamboyant, fruity, or gay-coded; used playfully or as a dig.
zesty.culturalNote: TikTok and Gen Z; can be affectionate or mean.

thirst trap.usageNote: A post meant to get attention or thirst. 'She posted a thirst trap.'

thirst.usageNote: Desperate for attention or validation. 'So much thirst.'

thirsty.usageNote: Desperate for attention, approval, or romance. 'He's so thirsty.'
thirsty.culturalNote: Dating and social media; Gen Z.

hot take.usageNote: A strong or controversial opinion. 'Hot take: that album is mid.'

cold take.usageNote: A boring or obvious opinion; opposite of hot take. 'That's a cold take.'

big yikes.usageNote: That's really bad or awkward. 'Big yikes.'

yikes.usageNote: Reaction to something awkward or bad. 'Yikes.'

oof.usageNote: Reaction to something painful or awkward. 'Oof.'

say less.usageNote: I understand; you don't need to say more. 'Say less, I'm in.'

I'm dead.usageNote: That's so funny or shocking. 'I'm dead.' 'I'm literally dead.'

we're so back.usageNote: Things are good again; we're winning. 'We're so back.'
we're so back.culturalNote: Twitter and meme culture; Gen Z.

it's so over.usageNote: Things are bad; we're done. 'It's so over.'
it's so over.culturalNote: Twitter and doomer meme; Gen Z.

doomer.usageNote: Someone pessimistic about the future. Doomer mindset.

bloomer.usageNote: Someone optimistic; opposite of doomer. 'I'm a bloomer.'

zoomer.culturalNote: Internet; Gen Z self-identify or are called zoomers.

okay boomer.usageNote: Dismissive reply to an out-of-touch take. 'Okay boomer.'

noob.usageNote: Newbie; someone bad or new at something. 'You're a noob.'

scrub.usageNote: Someone bad at the game; loser. 'Scrub.'

gg.usageNote: Good game; said at the end of a match or to concede. 'Gg.'

ez.usageNote: Easy; used to trash-talk after a win. 'Ez.'

pwned.usageNote: Owned; destroyed. 'You got pwned.' From leetspeak.

clout chaser.usageNote: Someone who does things just for fame or attention. 'Clout chaser.'

ded.usageNote: Dead; spelling for emphasis. 'I'm ded.' 'That killed me.'

newbie.usageNote: Someone new to something. 'She's a newbie.'

wp.usageNote: Well played; said after a good game or move. 'Wp.'

glhf.usageNote: Good luck have fun; said at the start of a game. 'Glhf.'

newb.usageNote: Short for newbie; someone new or bad. 'You're a newb.'

she ate.usageNote: She killed it; did amazing. 'She ate.'
she ate.culturalNote: Ballroom and Black English; Gen Z.

selfie.usageNote: A photo you take of yourself, usually with a phone. 'Take a selfie.'
selfie.culturalNote: 2010s social media; Millennial and Gen Z.

hashtag.usageNote: The # symbol used to tag topics on social media. 'Hashtag blessed.'

binge.usageNote: To do something in excess; binge-watch a show. 'I binged the whole season.'

binge-watch.usageNote: To watch many episodes of a show in one sitting. 'We binge-watched it.'

swipe left.usageNote: To reject someone on a dating app. 'I swiped left.'

swipe right.usageNote: To like or match with someone on a dating app. 'Swipe right.'

match.usageNote: When two people like each other on a dating app. 'We matched.'

brunch.usageNote: Late breakfast/early lunch; weekend meal. 'Let's get brunch.'

avocado toast.usageNote: Toast with mashed avocado; stereotype of Millennial spending. 'Avocado toast.'
avocado toast.culturalNote: Millennial stereotype; blamed for not buying houses.

mansplain.usageNote: When a man explains something condescendingly to a woman. 'Stop mansplaining.'

mansplaining.usageNote: Condescending explanation by a man to a woman. 'That's mansplaining.'

humblebrag.usageNote: Bragging while pretending to be humble. 'Ugh, my third vacation this year.'

first world problems.usageNote: Minor complaints that only rich countries have. 'First world problems.'

hot mess.usageNote: Chaotic but attractive; or a disaster. 'She's a hot mess.'
hot mess.culturalNote: Reality TV and pop culture; Millennial.

hot mess express.usageNote: Someone or something that's a total hot mess. 'Hot mess express.'

facepalm.usageNote: To put your hand on your face in disbelief. 'Facepalm.'

bae.usageNote: Babe; significant other or favorite. 'That's bae.'
bae.culturalNote: Before anyone else; Black English and Millennial.

queen.usageNote: Term of praise; you're the best. 'You're a queen.' 'Yas queen.'

real talk.usageNote: Seriously; being honest. 'Real talk, that was bad.'

vibes.usageNote: The mood or feeling. 'Good vibes.' 'Bad vibes.'
vibes.culturalNote: Hippie roots; Millennial and Gen Z.

mood.usageNote: I relate; that's how I feel. 'That's a mood.' 'Big mood.'

goals.usageNote: Something to aspire to. 'Relationship goals.' 'Life goals.'

life goals.usageNote: What I want in life. 'That's life goals.'

aesthetic.usageNote: A vibe or visual style. 'That's my aesthetic.'

hygge.usageNote: Danish concept of coziness and comfort. 'So hygge.'

self-care.usageNote: Taking care of yourself; me time. 'Self-care day.'

burnout.usageNote: Exhaustion from overwork or stress. 'I have burnout.'

hustle.usageNote: To work hard; grind. 'Side hustle.' 'Hustle culture.'

side hustle.usageNote: A side job or gig for extra money. 'I have a side hustle.'

gig.usageNote: A job or project, often short-term. 'Gig economy.' 'I have a gig.'

influencer.usageNote: Someone with a large social following who promotes products. 'She's an influencer.'

blog.usageNote: Web log; online journal or site. 'I have a blog.'

unboxing.usageNote: Video of opening a product. 'Unboxing video.'

haul.usageNote: Video or post showing stuff you bought. 'Haul video.' 'Shopping haul.'

flat lay.usageNote: Photo of items arranged flat from above. 'Flat lay.'

throwback.usageNote: Old photo or memory. 'Throwback to last summer.'

tbt.usageNote: Throwback Thursday; posting an old photo. 'Tbt to 2015.'

meme.usageNote: A viral image or idea. 'That's a meme.' 'Send me the meme.'
meme.culturalNote: Internet culture; Millennial and Gen Z.

trending.usageNote: Popular right now. 'It's trending on Twitter.'

catfish.usageNote: Someone who fakes their identity online. 'He's a catfish.'

catfishing.usageNote: Pretending to be someone else online. 'She's catfishing.'

unfriend.usageNote: To remove someone from your friends list. 'I unfriended him.'

unfollow.usageNote: To stop following someone. 'I unfollowed her.'

dm.usageNote: Direct message. 'Dm me.' 'Slide into my DMs.'

lol.usageNote: Laugh out loud; used when something's funny. 'Lol.'

lmao.usageNote: Laughing my ass off. 'Lmao that's great.'

hmu.usageNote: Hit me up; contact me. 'Hmu if you want to hang.'

netflix and chill.usageNote: Euphemism for hooking up while watching Netflix. 'Netflix and chill?'

booty call.usageNote: A late-night call or text for sex. 'It was a booty call.'

hookup.usageNote: Casual sex or romantic encounter. 'We hooked up.'

cuffing season.usageNote: Fall/winter when people want a relationship. 'It's cuffing season.'

ride or die.usageNote: Someone who's loyal no matter what. 'She's my ride or die.'

tribe.usageNote: Your close group of friends. 'My tribe.'

crew.usageNote: Your friend group or team. 'My crew.'

thot.usageNote: That hoe over there; derogatory for a woman. 'She's a thot.'
thot.culturalNote: Internet and hip-hop; Millennial; often criticized.

otp.usageNote: One true pairing; favorite fictional or real couple. 'They're my otp.'

bromance.usageNote: Close friendship between two guys. 'Their bromance.'

frenemy.usageNote: Friend who's also a rival. 'She's my frenemy.'

relationship goals.usageNote: A couple to aspire to. 'Relationship goals.'

career goals.usageNote: Job or career to aspire to. 'Career goals.'

no filter.usageNote: Being blunt; or a photo without a filter. 'No filter.'

read receipt.usageNote: Notification that someone read your message. 'Read receipts on.'

throwback thursday.usageNote: Posting an old photo on Thursday. 'Throwback Thursday.'

tl;dr.usageNote: Too long; didn't read. Summary. 'Tl;dr: it was great.'

imho.usageNote: In my humble opinion. 'Imho that's wrong.'

eli5.usageNote: Explain like I'm 5; simple explanation. 'Eli5: how does this work?'

blogger.usageNote: Someone who writes a blog. 'She's a blogger.'

seen.usageNote: When someone has read your message (read receipt). 'He left me on seen.'

big mood.usageNote: I strongly relate. 'That's a big mood.'

same.usageNote: I feel the same; me too. 'Same.' 'Same here.'

wtf.usageNote: What the fuck; expression of shock or confusion. 'Wtf.'

square.usageNote: Uncool, conventional, or straight-laced. 'Don't be a square.'

hep.usageNote: Hip; in the know. 'He's hep to the scene.'
hep.culturalNote: Jazz era; precursor to hip; Boomer.

hepcat.usageNote: A cool person; someone who's hep. 'That hepcat.'

pad.usageNote: Apartment or room. 'Come to my pad.'

bread.culturalNote: Rhyming slang (bread = bread and honey = money); Boomer.

bummer.usageNote: A downer; something disappointing. 'What a bummer.'

downer.usageNote: Something depressing; a buzzkill. 'That's a downer.'

trip.usageNote: Experience, especially on drugs; or a weird experience. 'Bad trip.'

freak out.usageNote: To panic, lose it, or have an intense reaction. 'Don't freak out.'

crash.usageNote: To sleep somewhere; or to show up. 'Can I crash at your place?'

joint.usageNote: A place; a bar or club. 'This joint.' 'Let's blow this joint.'

happening.usageNote: Event or scene; or cool, going on. 'Where's the happening?'

scene.usageNote: The social world or place. 'The scene.' 'Not my scene.'

jive.usageNote: Nonsense; to fool someone. 'Don't give me that jive.'

jive turkey.usageNote: Someone who talks jive; a fool. 'You jive turkey.'

turkey.usageNote: A loser or jerk. 'What a turkey.'

the man.usageNote: The establishment; authority. 'Stick it to the man.'

establishment.usageNote: The powers that be; mainstream authority. 'The establishment.'

narc.usageNote: Informant; narcotics officer. 'Don't be a narc.'

bust.usageNote: Arrest; or to catch someone. 'We got busted.'

rip off.usageNote: To cheat or steal. 'They ripped me off.'

power to the people.usageNote: Slogan of solidarity. 'Power to the people!'

stick it to the man.usageNote: To defy authority. 'Stick it to the man.'

soul brother.usageNote: Black man; brother in solidarity. 'Soul brother.'

soul sister.usageNote: Black woman; sister in solidarity. 'Soul sister.'

wig out.usageNote: To freak out; lose it. 'She wigged out.'

flip out.usageNote: To freak out; lose your cool. 'He flipped out.'

crash pad.usageNote: Place to sleep; temporary digs. 'Need a crash pad.'

blow this joint.usageNote: To leave this place. 'Let's blow this joint.'

hang.usageNote: To hang out; spend time. 'Let's hang.'

hang out.usageNote: To spend time together. 'We hung out.'

breadwinner.usageNote: Person who earns the family's money. 'She's the breadwinner.'

gas.usageNote: Something fun or hilarious. 'That was a gas.'

drag.usageNote: Something boring or a bummer. 'What a drag.'

buzz.usageNote: A high or good feeling. 'I've got a buzz.'

turn on.usageNote: To excite or attract; or to get high. 'That turns me on.'

turn off.usageNote: To repel or bore. 'That turns me off.'

turn on tune in drop out.headword: turn on tune in drop out
turn on tune in drop out.usageNote: Leary's slogan: get high, expand mind, leave mainstream.

high.usageNote: Under the influence of drugs. 'We got high.'

trip out.usageNote: To have a drug trip; or to be amazed. 'I'm tripping out.'

head.usageNote: Drug user or fan. 'He's a head.' 'Pothead.'

freak.usageNote: Enthusiast; or weirdo. 'He's a freak for jazz.'

gigging.usageNote: Playing gigs; doing paid performances. 'We're gigging tonight.'

bread and butter.usageNote: Main source of income. 'That's my bread and butter.'

bread and honey.usageNote: Rhyming slang for money. 'Bread and honey.'

can you dig it.usageNote: Do you understand? Do you get it? 'Can you dig it?'

keen.usageNote: Enthusiastic; great. 'I'm keen on it.' 'That's keen.'

gnarly.usageNote: Extreme; cool or gross. 'Gnarly wave.' 'That's gnarly.'

wicked.culturalNote: 80s East Coast and UK; Gen X.

slacker.usageNote: Someone who avoids work; slacker ethos.

diss.usageNote: To disrespect or insult. 'Don't diss me.'

dis.usageNote: Short for diss; to disrespect. 'He dissed me.'

gag me.usageNote: That's disgusting; I'm grossed out. 'Gag me with a spoon.'

loser.usageNote: A lame person; failure. 'What a loser.'

nerd.usageNote: Geek; uncool or later proudly so. 'Total nerd.'

spaz.usageNote: To freak out; or a clumsy person. 'Don't spaz.'

jam.usageNote: Song; or to hang. 'That jam.' 'Let's jam.'

homegirl.usageNote: Female friend from the neighborhood. 'My homegirl.'

random.usageNote: Random person or thing; weird. 'Some random.'

veg out.usageNote: To zone out; do nothing. 'Vegging out on the couch.'

zone out.usageNote: To space out; stop paying attention. 'I zoned out.'

spaced out.usageNote: Out of it; distracted. 'He's spaced out.'

screwed.usageNote: In trouble; cheated. 'I'm screwed.' 'We got screwed.'

cya.culturalNote: Early internet and chat; Gen X.

score.usageNote: To get something good; success. 'I scored.'

ace.usageNote: To nail it; do great. 'I aced it.'

down with.usageNote: In on; okay with. 'I'm down with that.'

all that.usageNote: All that and a bag of chips; the best. 'She's all that.'

don't have a cow.usageNote: Don't freak out. 'Don't have a cow.'

talk to the hand.usageNote: I'm not listening. 'Talk to the hand.'

mosh.usageNote: To slam dance. 'Moshing in the pit.'

rock out.usageNote: To party to rock; go hard. 'Let's rock out.'

valley girl.usageNote: Stereotype: California girl who says like, whatever, etc.

grody to the max.usageNote: Super gross. 'That's grody to the max.'

word to your mother.usageNote: Truth; I swear. 'Word to your mother.'

all that and a bag of chips.headword: all that and a bag of chips
all that and a bag of chips.usageNote: The best; full package. 'She's all that and a bag of chips.'

not.usageNote: Just kidding; the opposite. 'I'm so going... not!'

hello.usageNote: Standard greeting when meeting someone or answering the phone; from hullo, itself from holla (to shout).
hello.culturalNote: Universal in North American English; used across all generations.

word.usageNote: In Black English and casual use, 'word' can mean 'I agree' or 'that's true' (e.g. 'Word, I'm with you').
word.culturalNote: Also used as exclamation: 'Word up' or 'Word.' for affirmation.

language.usageNote: In casual and academic use; 'language' can refer to a particular variety (e.g. 'Black English', 'slang').
language.culturalNote: North American usage often distinguishes standard and vernacular varieties.
